design_philosophy.core_loop: Guide a marble through isometric maze courses within time limits, navigating obstacles and enemies while managing momentum and gravity-influenced physics
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with physics-based marble control difficulty; arcade-style risk/reward of speed versus accuracy
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Precision control of a rolling marble through surreal, visually distinct obstacle courses; competitive racing against time and opponents
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Minimalist visual design inspired by M.C. Escher; isometric perspective as core identity; trackball control in arcade prioritizes unique input over conventional gamepads; designer Mark Cerny deliberately chose to emphasize distinctive experience through control system novelty post-1983-crash; time-limit pressure as primary difficulty mechanism rather than lives

difficulty.lives_system: No traditional lives; time-based resource (each course has time limit)
difficulty.continues_system: Time carries over between courses (except first race); bonus time awarded for race completion and multiplayer wins
difficulty.checkpointing: Six distinct sequential races; no mid-race checkpoints; failure to complete within time ends run
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Single difficulty level with fixed time limits; no selectable difficulty modes documented; player can optimize routes and practice courses but cannot adjust challenge parameters

progression.unlock_mechanism: Six sequential courses unlock in order; first course (Practice) is tutorial-length, shorter and simpler than others; courses become progressively more difficult with additional obstacles and enemies
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer competitive mode with separate clocks and bonus time for winners
Distinct visual themes per course encourage replay to master specific layouts
High score tables with regional name variations

world_building_techniques: Distinct visual themes per course (e.g., fifth race 'Silly' features polka-dot patterns, opposite orientation)
M.C. Escher-inspired isometric perspective creates surreal, non-realistic environment
No dialogue or cutscenes; pure gameplay-driven experience

danger_signals: Increasingly difficult obstacle placement and enemy density as courses progress
Time pressure escalation creates psychological stress
environmental_storytelling: Isometric maze design with contours influencing marble physics path
Course-specific visual theming (polka-dot 'Silly' course, varied track surfaces)
Enemy and obstacle population density increases per course
